Serujan Satkunanathan
serujansatkunanathan@gmail.com +94755049713 Linkedin Github Portfolio SriLanka 
SUMMARY
I began as a Flutter developer, but users wanted apps that could think, not just run static code. That pushed me to learn LLMs,
vector databases, and retrieval systems. Iʼve been building with LangChain, experimenting with RAG, and connecting apps to
external data to give real, useful answers. Iʼm still learning, which is how I like to work.At Trivyol, I worked on a government
environmental project with constantly changing requirements. I learned to understand stakeholder needs and translate them
clearly to the backend team, bridging the gap between people and technology.
PROFESSIONAL EXPERIENCE
Associate Mobile Application Developer, Trivyol Inc
•Contributed to Apaluma, a government-backed platform focused on transforming 
environmental data into actionable intelligence for public and institutional use. 
•Developed a location-based mobile application using Flutter with Google Maps integration to 
provide real-time weather and environmental insights. 05/2025 – 02/2026
•Implemented live weather updates, continuous location tracking, and state management 
using Provider for scalable and maintainable performance. 
•Collaborated with designers, backend engineers, and government stakeholders to deliver a 
responsive, intuitive, and data-driven user experience, aligned with national environmental 
data standards and public sector design principles.
Mobile Application Developer Intern, AUS-Etech
•Developed Crush Date, a cross-platform dating app in Flutter, with features including chat, 
user profiles.
•Built Task Chase, a freelancer-like app, where I developed the entire UI and integrated user 
authentication with backend.11/2024 – 05/2025
•Developed and maintained backend services using Laravel and Firebase (authentication, real-
time database, APIs).
•Created and customized an admin panel to manage users and content.
•Supported branding efforts by designing logos and marketing materials.
•Also spent time documenting how the APIs worked so the team had clear references.
